remedies.title: Mediterranean Table Tradition
instructions: Cook with olive oil instead of butter or hard fats.
Build meals around vegetables, beans, and whole grains, with meat as a side rather than the centre.
Eat fish a couple of times a week; keep red meat occasional.
Finish meals with fruit rather than sweets.
Eat with others when you can — the shared table is part of the tradition.
remedies.origin: The everyday eating pattern of Crete, southern Italy, and coastal Spain as documented in the mid-20th century.
remedies.disclaimer: A dietary pattern, not a treatment. Adapt with a doctor or dietitian if you have a medical condition.

remedies.title: Daily Fermented Foods
instructions: Add one small serving of a fermented food to a meal each day.
Rotate between dairy, vegetable, and grain ferments through the week.
Choose raw, unpasteurised ferments where safe and available — heat kills the live cultures.
Introduce slowly if you are not used to them.
remedies.origin: Nearly every food culture keeps a ferment: kimchi in Korea, sauerkraut in Central Europe, ogi in Nigeria, laban across the Levant.
remedies.disclaimer: People with weakened immune systems should ask a doctor before eating unpasteurised ferments.

remedies.title: Bitter Greens Before Meals

ingredients.name: bitter leaves (arugula, dandelion, endive, or African bitterleaf, well prepared)

instructions: Start the main meal with a small salad of bitter greens dressed simply with oil and lemon.
Chew slowly — the bitter taste itself triggers digestive juices.
Make it a habit at your largest meal of the day.
remedies.origin: From Italian 'amaro' aperitifs to Nigerian bitterleaf soup, traditions worldwide open digestion with bitterness.
remedies.disclaimer: Wild-foraged greens must be identified with certainty; when in doubt, use market greens.

remedies.title: Eat-the-Rainbow Plate

ingredients.name: vegetables and fruits of at least 4 different colours

instructions: At each main meal, glance at your plate and count the natural colours.
Aim for four or more colours across the day: leafy green, red/orange, purple/blue, white/yellow.
Shop seasonally — colour variety follows the seasons in traditional markets.
remedies.origin: A modern phrasing of an old market-day instinct: a colourful basket is a nourishing basket.
remedies.disclaimer: General guidance, not a treatment plan.
